content: Notification badge count desync pitfalls: (1) Badge count is derived from WebSocket events only — when tab is closed and reopened, badge is 0 because missed events were never received; must initialize badge count from a REST endpoint on page load, then delta-update from WS. (2) Multi-tab count divergence — Tab A receives WS event and increments badge; Tab B is open but gets its own WS connection with the same event; both show +1 but unread count should only be +1; requires server-authoritative count or BroadcastChannel for cross-tab sync. (3) Read-receipt race — user reads notification in Tab A; Tab B's badge stays stale because the read event was only applied locally; must send read event to server which broadcasts to all connections for that user. (4) Reconnect gap — exponential backoff means 30+ seconds of missed events; buffered replay fills the gap but must be idempotent to avoid double-counting.
